root_text.sn51.10:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā vesāliyaṁ viharati mahāvane kūṭāgārasālāyaṁ.
root_text.sn51.10:1.3: Atha kho bhagavā pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya vesāliṁ piṇḍāya pāvisi.
root_text.sn51.10:1.4: Vesāliyaṁ piṇḍāya caritvā pacchābhattaṁ piṇḍapātapaṭikkanto āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ āmantesi:
root_text.sn51.10:1.7: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho āyasmā ānando bhagavato paṭissutvā nisīdanaṁ ādāya bhagavantaṁ piṭṭhito piṭṭhito anubandhi.
root_text.sn51.10:2.1: Atha kho bhagavā yena cāpālaṁ cetiyaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi.
root_text.sn51.10:2.2: Āyasmāpi kho ānando bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi.
root_text.sn51.10:2.3: Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.sn51.10:3.1: “Ramaṇīyā, ānanda, vesālī, ramaṇīyaṁ udenaṁ cetiyaṁ, ramaṇīyaṁ gotamakaṁ cetiyaṁ, ramaṇīyaṁ sattambaṁ cetiyaṁ, ramaṇīyaṁ bahuputtaṁ cetiyaṁ, ramaṇīyaṁ sārandadaṁ cetiyaṁ, ramaṇīyaṁ cāpālaṁ cetiyaṁ.
root_text.sn51.10:3.2: Yassa kassaci, ānanda, cattāro iddhipādā bhāvitā bahulīkatā yānīkatā vatthukatā anuṭṭhitā paricitā susamāraddhā, so ākaṅkhamāno kappaṁ vā tiṭṭheyya kappāvasesaṁ vā.
root_text.sn51.10:3.3: Tathāgatassa kho, ānanda, cattāro iddhipādā bhāvitā bahulīkatā yānīkatā vatthukatā anuṭṭhitā paricitā susamāraddhā. Ākaṅkhamāno, ānanda, tathāgato kappaṁ vā tiṭṭheyya kappāvasesaṁ vā”ti.
root_text.sn51.10:4.1: Evampi kho āyasmā ānando bhagavatā oḷārike nimitte kayiramāne oḷārike obhāse kayiramāne nāsakkhi paṭivijjhituṁ;
root_text.sn51.10:4.3: “tiṭṭhatu, bhante, bhagavā kappaṁ, tiṭṭhatu sugato kappaṁ bahujanahitāya bahujanasukhāya lokānukampāya atthāya hitāya sukhāya devamanussānan”ti yathā taṁ mārena pariyuṭṭhitacitto.
root_text.sn51.10:5.2: tatiyampi kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ āmantesi:
root_text.sn51.10:5.3: “ramaṇīyā, ānanda, vesālī, ramaṇīyaṁ udenaṁ cetiyaṁ, ramaṇīyaṁ gotamakaṁ cetiyaṁ, ramaṇīyaṁ sattambaṁ cetiyaṁ, ramaṇīyaṁ bahuputtaṁ cetiyaṁ, ramaṇīyaṁ sārandadaṁ cetiyaṁ, ramaṇīyaṁ cāpālaṁ cetiyaṁ.
root_text.sn51.10:5.4: Yassa kassaci, ānanda, cattāro iddhipādā bhāvitā bahulīkatā yānīkatā vatthukatā anuṭṭhitā paricitā susamāraddhā, so ākaṅkhamāno kappaṁ vā tiṭṭheyya kappāvasesaṁ vā.
root_text.sn51.10:5.5: Tathāgatassa kho, ānanda, cattāro iddhipādā bhāvitā bahulīkatā yānīkatā vatthukatā anuṭṭhitā paricitā susamāraddhā. Ākaṅkhamāno, ānanda, tathāgato kappaṁ vā tiṭṭheyya kappāvasesaṁ vā”ti.
root_text.sn51.10:6.1: Evampi kho āyasmā ānando bhagavatā oḷārike nimitte kayiramāne oḷārike obhāse kayiramāne nāsakkhi paṭivijjhituṁ;
root_text.sn51.10:6.3: “tiṭṭhatu, bhante, bhagavā kappaṁ, tiṭṭhatu sugato kappaṁ bahujanahitāya bahujanasukhāya lokānukampāya atthāya hitāya sukhāya devamanussānan”ti yathā taṁ mārena pariyuṭṭhitacitto.
root_text.sn51.10:7.1: Atha kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ āmantesi:
root_text.sn51.10:7.4: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho āyasmā ānando bhagavato paṭissutvā uṭṭhāyāsanā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā avidūre aññatarasmiṁ rukkhamūle nisīdi.
root_text.sn51.10:8.1: Atha kho māro pāpimā, acirapakkante āyasmante ānande, yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn51.10:9.1: “Parinibbātu dāni, bhante, bhagavā, parinibbātu dāni sugato. Parinibbānakālo dāni, bhante, bhagavato.
root_text.sn51.10:9.2: Bhāsitā kho panesā, bhante, bhagavatā vācā:
root_text.sn51.10:9.3: ‘na tāvāhaṁ, pāpima, parinibbāyissāmi yāva me bhikkhū na sāvakā bhavissanti viyattā vinītā visāradā bahussutā dhammadharā dhammānudhammappaṭipannā sāmīcippaṭipannā anudhammacārino, sakaṁ ācariyakaṁ uggahetvā ācikkhissanti desessanti paññapessanti paṭṭhapessanti vivarissanti vibhajissanti uttānīkarissanti, uppannaṁ parappavādaṁ sahadhammena suniggahitaṁ niggahetvā sappāṭihāriyaṁ dhammaṁ desessantī’ti.
root_text.sn51.10:10.1: Santi kho pana, bhante, etarahi bhikkhū bhagavato sāvakā viyattā vinītā visāradā bahussutā dhammadharā dhammānudhammappaṭipannā sāmīcippaṭipannā anudhammacārino, sakaṁ ācariyakaṁ uggahetvā ācikkhanti desenti paññapenti paṭṭhapenti vivaranti vibhajanti uttānīkaronti, uppannaṁ parappavādaṁ sahadhammena suniggahitaṁ niggahetvā sappāṭihāriyaṁ dhammaṁ desenti.
root_text.sn51.10:10.2: Parinibbātu dāni, bhante, bhagavā, parinibbātu dāni, sugato. Parinibbānakālo dāni, bhante, bhagavato.
root_text.sn51.10:11.1: Bhāsitā kho panesā, bhante, bhagavatā vācā:
root_text.sn51.10:11.2: ‘na tāvāhaṁ, pāpima, parinibbāyissāmi yāva me bhikkhuniyo na sāvikā bhavissanti viyattā vinītā visāradā bahussutā dhammadharā dhammānudhammappaṭipannā sāmīcippaṭipannā anudhammacāriniyo, sakaṁ ācariyakaṁ uggahetvā ācikkhissanti desessanti paññapessanti paṭṭhapessanti vivarissanti vibhajissanti uttānīkarissanti, uppannaṁ parappavādaṁ sahadhammena suniggahitaṁ niggahetvā sappāṭihāriyaṁ dhammaṁ desessantī’ti.
root_text.sn51.10:12.1: Santi kho pana, bhante, etarahi bhikkhuniyo bhagavato sāvikā viyattā vinītā visāradā bahussutā dhammadharā dhammānudhammappaṭipannā sāmīcippaṭipannā anudhammacāriniyo, sakaṁ ācariyakaṁ uggahetvā ācikkhanti desenti paññapenti paṭṭhapenti vivaranti vibhajanti uttānīkaronti, uppannaṁ parappavādaṁ sahadhammena suniggahitaṁ niggahetvā sappāṭihāriyaṁ dhammaṁ desenti.
root_text.sn51.10:12.2: Parinibbātu dāni, bhante, bhagavā, parinibbātu dāni, sugato. Parinibbānakālo dāni, bhante, bhagavato.
root_text.sn51.10:13.1: Bhāsitā kho panesā, bhante, bhagavatā vācā:
root_text.sn51.10:13.2: ‘na tāvāhaṁ, pāpima, parinibbāyissāmi yāva me upāsakā …pe…
root_text.sn51.10:13.3: yāva me upāsikā na sāvikā bhavissanti viyattā vinītā visāradā bahussutā dhammadharā dhammānudhammappaṭipannā sāmīcippaṭipannā anudhammacāriniyo, sakaṁ ācariyakaṁ uggahetvā ācikkhissanti desessanti paññapessanti paṭṭhapessanti vivarissanti vibhajissanti uttānīkarissanti, uppannaṁ parappavādaṁ sahadhammena suniggahitaṁ niggahetvā sappāṭihāriyaṁ dhammaṁ desessantī’ti.
root_text.sn51.10:14.1: Santi kho pana, bhante, etarahi upāsakā …
root_text.sn51.10:14.2: upāsikā bhagavato sāvikā viyattā vinītā visāradā bahussutā dhammadharā dhammānudhammappaṭipannā sāmīcippaṭipannā anudhammacāriniyo, sakaṁ ācariyakaṁ uggahetvā ācikkhanti desenti paññapenti paṭṭhapenti vivaranti vibhajanti uttānīkaronti, uppannaṁ parappavādaṁ sahadhammena suniggahitaṁ niggahetvā sappāṭihāriyaṁ dhammaṁ desenti.
root_text.sn51.10:14.3: Parinibbātu dāni, bhante, bhagavā, parinibbātu dāni, sugato. Parinibbānakālo dāni, bhante, bhagavato.
root_text.sn51.10:15.1: Bhāsitā kho panesā, bhante, bhagavatā vācā:
root_text.sn51.10:15.2: ‘na tāvāhaṁ, pāpima, parinibbāyissāmi yāva me idaṁ brahmacariyaṁ na iddhañceva bhavissati phītañca vitthāritaṁ bāhujaññaṁ puthubhūtaṁ yāva devamanussehi suppakāsitan’ti.
root_text.sn51.10:15.3: Tayidaṁ, bhante, bhagavato brahmacariyaṁ iddhañceva phītañca vitthāritaṁ bāhujaññaṁ puthubhūtaṁ yāva devamanussehi suppakāsitaṁ.
root_text.sn51.10:15.4: Parinibbātu dāni, bhante, bhagavā, parinibbātu dāni sugato. Parinibbānakālo dāni, bhante, bhagavato”ti.
root_text.sn51.10:16.1: Evaṁ vutte, bhagavā māraṁ pāpimantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn51.10:16.2: “appossukko tvaṁ, pāpima, hohi. Na ciraṁ tathāgatassa parinibbānaṁ bhavissati.
root_text.sn51.10:16.3: Ito tiṇṇaṁ māsānaṁ accayena tathāgato parinibbāyissatī”ti.
root_text.sn51.10:17.1: Atha kho bhagavā cāpāle cetiye sato sampajāno āyusaṅkhāraṁ ossaji.
root_text.sn51.10:17.2: Ossaṭṭhe ca bhagavatā āyusaṅkhāre mahābhūmicālo ahosi bhiṁsanako lomahaṁso, devadundubhiyo ca phaliṁsu.
root_text.sn51.10:17.3: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.sn51.10:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Vesālī, at the Great Wood, in the hall with the peaked roof.
translation_text.sn51.10:1.3: Then the Buddha robed up in the morning and, taking his bowl and robe, entered Vesālī for alms.
translation_text.sn51.10:1.4: Then, after the meal, on his return from almsround, he addressed Venerable Ānanda:
translation_text.sn51.10:1.6: Let’s go to the Cāpāla Shrine for the day’s meditation.”
translation_text.sn51.10:1.7: “Yes, sir,” replied Ānanda. Taking his sitting cloth he followed behind the Buddha.
translation_text.sn51.10:2.1: Then the Buddha went up to the Cāpāla Shrine, and sat down on the seat spread out.
translation_text.sn51.10:2.2: Ānanda bowed to the Buddha and sat down to one side.
translation_text.sn51.10:3.1: “Ānanda, Vesālī is lovely. And the Udena, Gotamaka, Seven Maidens, Many Sons, Sārandada, and Cāpāla Shrines are all lovely.
translation_text.sn51.10:3.2: Whoever has developed and cultivated the four bases of psychic power—made them a vehicle and a basis, kept them up, consolidated them, and properly implemented them—may, if they wish, live for the proper lifespan or what’s left of it.
translation_text.sn51.10:3.3: The Realized One has developed and cultivated the four bases of psychic power, made them a vehicle and a basis, kept them up, consolidated them, and properly implemented them. If he wished, the Realized One could live for the proper lifespan or what’s left of it.”
translation_text.sn51.10:4.1: But Ānanda didn’t get it, even though the Buddha dropped such an obvious hint, such a clear sign.
translation_text.sn51.10:4.3: “Sir, may the Blessed One please remain for the proper lifespan! May the Holy One please remain for the proper lifespan! That would be for the welfare and happiness of the people, out of sympathy for the world, for the benefit, welfare, and happiness of gods and humans.” For his mind was as if possessed by Māra.
translation_text.sn51.10:5.2: and for a third time, the Buddha said to Ānanda:
translation_text.sn51.10:5.3: “Ānanda, Vesālī is lovely. And the Udena, Gotamaka, Seven Maidens, Many Sons, Sārandada, and Cāpāla Shrines are all lovely.
translation_text.sn51.10:5.4: Whoever has developed and cultivated the four bases of psychic power—made them a vehicle and a basis, kept them up, consolidated them, and properly implemented them—may, if they wish, live for the proper lifespan or what’s left of it.
translation_text.sn51.10:5.5: The Realized One has developed and cultivated the four bases of psychic power, made them a vehicle and a basis, kept them up, consolidated them, and properly implemented them. If he wished, the Realized One could live for the proper lifespan or what’s left of it.”
translation_text.sn51.10:6.1: But Ānanda didn’t get it, even though the Buddha dropped such an obvious hint, such a clear sign.
translation_text.sn51.10:6.3: “Sir, may the Blessed One please remain for the proper lifespan! May the Holy One please remain for the proper lifespan! That would be for the welfare and happiness of the people, out of sympathy for the world, for the benefit, welfare, and happiness of gods and humans.” For his mind was as if possessed by Māra.
translation_text.sn51.10:7.1: Then the Buddha said to him,
translation_text.sn51.10:7.4: “Yes, sir,” replied Ānanda. He rose from his seat, bowed, and respectfully circled the Buddha, keeping him on his right, before sitting at the root of a tree close by.
translation_text.sn51.10:8.1: And then, not long after Ānanda had left, Māra the Wicked went up to the Buddha and said to him:
translation_text.sn51.10:9.1: “Sir, may the Blessed One now be fully extinguished! May the Holy One now be fully extinguished! Now is the time for the full extinguishment of the Buddha.
translation_text.sn51.10:9.2: Sir, you once made this statement:
translation_text.sn51.10:9.3: ‘Wicked One, I shall not be fully extinguished until I have monk disciples who are competent, educated, assured, learned, have memorized the teachings, and practice in line with the teaching. Not until they practice properly, living in line with the teaching. Not until they’ve learned their own tradition, and explain, teach, assert, establish, disclose, analyze, and make it clear. Not until they can legitimately and completely refute the doctrines of others that come up, and teach with a demonstrable basis.’
translation_text.sn51.10:10.1: Today you do have such monk disciples.
translation_text.sn51.10:10.2: May the Blessed One now be fully extinguished! May the Holy One now be fully extinguished! Now is the time for the full extinguishment of the Buddha.
translation_text.sn51.10:11.1: Sir, you once made this statement:
translation_text.sn51.10:11.2: ‘Wicked One, I shall not be fully extinguished until I have nun disciples who are competent, educated, assured, learned …’ …
translation_text.sn51.10:12.1: Today you do have such nun disciples.
translation_text.sn51.10:12.2: May the Blessed One now be fully extinguished! May the Holy One now be fully extinguished! Now is the time for the full extinguishment of the Buddha.
translation_text.sn51.10:13.1: Sir, you once made this statement:
translation_text.sn51.10:13.2: ‘Wicked One, I shall not be fully extinguished until I have layman disciples …
translation_text.sn51.10:13.3: and laywoman disciples who are competent, educated, assured, learned …’ …
translation_text.sn51.10:14.1: Today you do have such layman
translation_text.sn51.10:14.3: May the Blessed One now be fully extinguished! May the Holy One now be fully extinguished! Now is the time for the full extinguishment of the Buddha.
translation_text.sn51.10:15.1: Sir, you once made this statement:
translation_text.sn51.10:15.2: ‘Wicked One, I shall not be fully extinguished until my spiritual path is successful and prosperous, extensive, popular, widespread, and well proclaimed wherever there are gods and humans.’
translation_text.sn51.10:15.3: Today your spiritual path is successful and prosperous, extensive, popular, widespread, and well proclaimed wherever there are gods and humans.
translation_text.sn51.10:15.4: May the Blessed One now be fully extinguished! May the Holy One now be fully extinguished! Now is the time for the full extinguishment of the Buddha.”
translation_text.sn51.10:16.1: When this was said, the Buddha said to Māra,
translation_text.sn51.10:16.2: “Relax, Wicked One. The full extinguishment of the Realized One will be soon.
translation_text.sn51.10:16.3: Three months from now the Realized One will be fully extinguished.”
translation_text.sn51.10:17.1: So at the Cāpāla Shrine the Buddha, mindful and aware, surrendered the life force.
translation_text.sn51.10:17.2: When he did so there was a great earthquake, awe-inspiring and hair-raising, and thunder cracked the sky.
translation_text.sn51.10:17.3: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.sn51.10:18.1: “Comparing the incomparable <j>with the creation of prolonged life,
translation_text.sn51.10:18.2: the sage surrendered the life force.
translation_text.sn51.10:18.4: he shattered self-creation like a suit of armor.”

variant_text.sn51.10:3.1: bahuputtaṁ cetiyaṁ → bahuputtakacetiyaṁ (sya-all, km, pts1ed, mr) | sārandadaṁ cetiyaṁ → sārandadacetiyaṁ (sya-all); ānandacetiyaṁ (mr); sānandaraṁ (katthaci)
variant_text.sn51.10:9.1: parinibbātu dāni sugato → parinibbātu sugato (bj, sya-all, km)
variant_text.sn51.10:17.2: Ossaṭṭhe ca → ossaṭṭhe pana (pts1ed); osajje pana (mr) | devadundubhiyo → devadudrabhiyo (mr)
